{{Quote|Still, the thought of 10,000 credits for just one tresher egg, delivered to a certain biologist on Deminol, seemed like a good deal at the time.|Extract from a confession by Fendel Ramison following his arrest by Tinallis authorities|<ref name="Creatures">{{WEGCite|story=The Ride Of A Lifetime|book=Creatures of the Galaxy}}</ref>}}
'''Deminol''' was a terrestrial<ref name="Creatures" /> [[Planet/Legends|world]] located in the [[Deminol system/Legends|Deminol system]] of the [[Juvex sector/Legends|Juvex sector]], a part of the [[Senex-Juvex/Legends|Senex-Juvex]] region in the [[Western Reaches/Legends|Western Reaches]] portion of the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]]. The [[Senex-Juvex Loop]] [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]] linked it to the [[Anstares system/Legends|Anstares]] and [[Ossiathora system/Legends|Ossiathora systems]].<ref name="TEA" />

Deminol was a bustling [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]] world, one of the busiest in the Juvex [[Sector/Legends|sector]], and, like the rest of the Senex-Juvex worlds, was under the control of the region's [[Noble/Legends|aristocratic]] [[Ancient Houses]]. It housed several [[University/Legends|universities]], which were attended by students from all of the Senex-Juvex Houses.<ref name="TEA" />

During the [[Imperial Era/Legends|reign]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], a [[Unidentified biologist (Deminol)|biologist]] on Deminol recruited [[Fendel Ramison]] to retrieve a single [[Egg/Legends|egg]] of the [[tresher]], a [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Bird/Legends|avians]] [[Homeworld/Legends|native]] to the planet [[Tinallis]], and deliver it to her. Ramison's attempt was ultimately unsuccessful, and he was arrested by the authorities on Tinallis for the violation of planetary conservation laws. In his confession, Ramison mentioned the [[Biologist/Legends|biologist]] on Deminol.<ref name="Creatures" />

==Behind the scenes==
Deminol was first mentioned in "[[The Ride Of A Lifetime]]," a short companion narrative authored by [[Rick D. Stuart]] and included in the [[1994]] sourcebook ''[[Creatures of the Galaxy]]'', published by [[West End Games]] for ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="Creatures" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the world in grid square L-17.<ref name="TEA" />
